title: gaultheria eriophylla var. eriophylla
intro: Gaultheria eriophylla var. eriophylla is a species of flowering plant in the heath family, Ericaceae, native to western North America. The plant's leaves and stems contain a compound called usnic acid, which is responsible for its medicinal properties. The medicinal properties of Gaultheria eriophylla var. eriophylla include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for treating various health issues, such as wounds, fever, and respiratory problems. Gaultheria eriophylla var. eriophylla can be prepared into various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and salves, which can be used to harness its health benefits.
